title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs seeded simulation, input replay, state hashes, settings, and save migration from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reproducible replay and versioned local state after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.
Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Rng Streams
steps.instruction:

content.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 8019 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss. Artifact under review: reproducible replay and versioned local state.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Schema Migration, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Schema Migration
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Schema Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 6302 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Schema Migration.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Schema Migration, keep Schema Migration connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Schema Migration, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A review Asteroids team handles case 6302 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Schema Migration, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Adding defaults to a dictionary is a complete save migration strategy.
